commit.message:
Carve tools: smooth curves, variable-width pressure ribbons, input smoothing

Three improvements to make Surface Pen carving feel natural:

1. Catmull-Rom curve fitting  replaces M/L polylines with smooth
   cubic Bezier curves (C commands) through all stroke points.
   Endpoints use reflection for smooth entry/exit.

2. Variable-width ribbon fills  replaces constant-width stroked
   segments with filled shapes whose width varies per-point based
   on pressure. Smooth Catmull-Rom curves on both ribbon edges.
   Improved pressure curve (power function) for better light-touch
   response.

3. Real-time input smoothing  exponential moving average (alpha=0.35
   for position, 0.5 for pressure) applied during pointermove to
   eliminate jitter. Carve ring cursor tracks raw position.

Also: lowered Douglas-Peucker tolerance (1.5  0.8) to retain more
curve detail now that Catmull-Rom smooths the rendering.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Migrate all tests from JUnit 4 to JUnit 5/6 (#715)

* Migrate all tests from JUnit 4 to JUnit 5/6

This comprehensive migration updates 214 test files to be compatible
with openmrs-core 3.0.0-SNAPSHOT which removed JUnit 4 support.

Changes include:
- Replace all JUnit 4 imports with JUnit 5 equivalents
  (org.junit.Test -> org.junit.jupiter.api.Test, etc.)
- Convert @Before/@After to @BeforeEach/@AfterEach
- Convert @BeforeClass to @BeforeAll
- Convert @Ignore to @Disabled
- Migrate Assert.xxx() to Assertions.xxx()
- Migrate assertThat to org.hamcrest.MatcherAssert.assertThat
- Convert all @Test(expected=X.class) to assertThrows()
- Convert ExpectedException @Rule to assertThrows() with message assertions
- Rewrite OpenmrsProfileRule from JUnit 4 TestRule to JUnit 5 ExecutionCondition
- Fix message-first assertion argument ordering (JUnit 4 message-first
  to JUnit 5 message-last)
- Update base class imports from org.openmrs.web.test.BaseModuleWebContextSensitiveTest
  to org.openmrs.web.test.jupiter.BaseModuleWebContextSensitiveTest
- Update BaseContextMockTest to jupiter package
- Add junit-bom:6.0.3 to POM dependencyManagement
- Update maven-surefire-plugin to 3.5.5

All 1760 tests pass with 0 failures and 0 errors.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove \${revisionNumber} from omod finalName to fix CI checksum step

The shared CI workflow (build-backend-module.yml) uses
mvn help:evaluate -Dexpression=project.build.finalName to determine the
omod filename, but help:evaluate does not execute lifecycle phases. The
buildnumber-maven-plugin sets \${revisionNumber} during validate, so it
resolves to null in help:evaluate, causing the "No such file" error.

fix(databases): handle 429 status code by returning an empty response and setting cache

fix(rate-limiter): remove 429-specific cache handling from apps and databases routes

Previously, when the Dokku API returned a 429 rate limit error, the routes
would cache an empty array for 5 seconds and return an empty response. This
workaround masked rate limiting issues from users.

Now rate limit errors are treated as regular errors and properly returned to
the client with the appropriate status code.

* fix(apps): use Running key for status, not Deployed key

The Deployed key indicates if an app has ever been deployed, not whether
it's currently running. Use only the Running key to correctly determine
if an app is running or stopped.

The delete operation now uses Server-Sent Events streaming, so the E2E test
needs to mock SSE responses instead of JSON. Added fulfillSSE helper to
route-utils.ts and updated the delete test to use it.

commit.message:
Update to .NET 10, XHarness 11.0, Xcode 26.2, and CI overhaul (#93)

## Framework & Tooling

- Target .NET 10 (multi-targeting net9.0 + net10.0)
- Update XHarness to 11.0.0-prerelease.26107.1
- Update Xcode to 26.2, Android API 36
- Pin MAUI workloads to 10.0.20 (net10) / 9.0.120 (net9)
- Update runners: windows-2025, macos-15-intel, ubuntu-24.04

## NuGet Package Updates

- Spectre.Console.Cli 0.49.1 → 0.53.1 (breaking: Execute requires CancellationToken)
- Spectre.Console.Testing 0.49.1 → 0.54.0 (breaking: CommandAppTester removed)
- Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk → 18.3.0
- coverlet.collector → 8.0.0, xunit.runner.visualstudio → 3.1.5
- NUnit → 4.5.1, NUnit.Analyzers → 4.12.0, NUnit3TestAdapter → 6.1.0
- NSubstitute → 5.3.0, CommunityToolkit.Mvvm → 8.4.0
- Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.* → 10.0.3

## CI Architecture Overhaul

### GitHub Actions
- Single ci.yml with matrix strategies for build (3 configs) and test (2 OS)
- 10 composite actions for device tests under .github/workflows/test-*/
- Pack job with CI versioning (0.999-ci.$RUN_ID) for PR validation
- Separate release.yml for NuGet publishing (security isolation)
- NuGet Trusted Publishing via OIDC (NuGet/login@v1) — no long-lived API keys

### Azure Pipelines
- Single azure-pipelines.yml with inlined build/test/pack (matrix strategy)
- 10 job templates under .azure/templates/test-*.yml

### CI Improvements
- Removed all timeout-minutes / timeoutInMinutes overrides
- Removed --cores, --partition-size, --timeout from emulator boot
- Parameterized --response-threshold for emulator stability
- Added validation guards for .app/.msix file searches (fail-fast on empty)
- Search only artifacts/ for build outputs (removed hardcoded sample paths)
- validate-arch composite action on all non-Windows device test jobs

## Submodule Removal

- Removed AndroidSdk.Tools submodule → NuGet androidsdk.tool
- Removed AppleDev.Tools submodule → NuGet appledev.tools
- Cleaned up .gitmodules, .git/config, .git/modules/

## Source Code Changes

- Added CancellationToken parameter to all 14 CLI command Execute methods
- Created local CommandAppTester shim (Spectre.Console.Testing 0.54 removed it)
- Fixed TestConsole width for JSON output tests (Profile.Width = 10000)
- Multi-targeted DeviceRunners.Cli for net9.0 + net10.0

## Documentation

- Rewrote docs/ci-pipeline.md to reflect actual architecture
